title: My security camera shipped a GitHub admin token in its login page
excerpt: i dissected some firmware for a Hanwha Wisenet XNP-9300RW and found that it had github admin tokens in it, also miltech is weird
content_html:
i have been thinking a bit more about security cameras again, because of AXIS starting to push more for every one of their cameras to be able to easily run linux applications on them, they're far more serious targets in an enterprise environment and need to be managed as such for vulnerabilities and credential management etc. someone brought up to me a company that sounded new to me, Hanwha (Vision.) I took a look at the site, and found that they had accessible firmware blobs for each model of camera, which is always a treat.

poking and prodding

i took the image and threw it at binwalk hoping it was just a rootfs or something, but inside there was a separate tarball with some AI stuff for the camera and a fwimage.tgz that binwalk was flagging as encrypted.

I was googling around and saw that Matt Brown has a writeup on these cameras that got me through. basically the passphrase is HTW + the model number so HTWXNP-9300RW worked.

seems like they do some more stuff now, because inside of that tarball was another fwimage.tgz that was encrypted but it wasn't the same scheme, so we can't just re-use the same setup from Matt Brown. I kinda figured I was gonna have to give up and that Hanwha was doing something more advanced, like burning a key into the hardware (which isnt foolproof obviously but you at least need to own the camera to start.) Anyways, there was a fwupgrader binary that was in that outer tarball, so I threw it into ghidra and started poking around.

well I would have been poking around if it was 2023 or something, but i pointed claude code at it and went to make a lovely dinner and spent time with my partner instead, and came back a bit later to a description and a nice rootfs.

Hanwha had built some obfuscation into the fwupgrader to hide how they decrypt the actual rootfs. the AES key is XOR'd against a small static key table in the binary and reassembled at runtime ( the IV is just plaintext in there) the fwupgrader just shells out to the openssl CLI, and even the command fragments are XOR-obfuscated the same way.

reconstructed the command looks like this:

openssl enc -md sha256 -aes-256-cbc -d \
  -K <KEY> -iv <IV> -in <INPUT> -out <OUTPUT>

Since the key and iv are just hardcoded (the same across the model line), I will publish them here:

KEY = dfa049bb922e63e2decc764af5628068e5b7a2662e479a615b14643e567579b0
IV  = 53f926801b81454a4f889c9a390db6e6

and with that we have a full rootfs to dig into normally.

truffles

since we finally can just look at stuff I ran trufflehog immediately to see if there was anything obvious, and there was a github token duplicated in like 30 files... I checked what repos the token had access to, and it had admin privileges to hundreds of repositories in their github organization.

this isn't my first rodeo with an org shipping a Github token in their firmware though... but that's a story for a different blog post. Why would this org put this token in like 30 files though? it looks like they build the UI for these cameras with vite, and one of the variables is being set to the entirety of process.env at build time, which means the entirety of the CI job's environment is being written to these files.

var W = {
  DATAPORT: "9090",
  GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE: "1",
  npm_command: "run-script",
  KUBERNETES_SERVICE_PORT_HTTPS: "443",
  GITHUB_NPM_TOKEN: "<snip>:ghp_…REDACTED…",
  npm_config_userconfig: "/home/docker/.npmrc",
  // etc

I don't have any of these cameras to test, but I think this would mean that anyone accessing the admin ui of these cameras likely has had this github token sent to them over the wire and (hopefully) nobody evil noticed. Maybe it didn't get actually served and just lived on disk, though.

there were some other... interesting bits of data in the environment though: there were some env vars with IP addresses in them, but they are assigned to the US Department of Defense:

  • SWARM_MASTER_NFS_ADDRESS: 55.101.212.23
  • OTEL_ELASTIC_URL: http://55.101.212.21:5601/<snip>
  • CIMIP: 55.101.211.213

huh... is this just a coincidence and one of those weird instances where people have taken IP space for internal services when they know they will never interact with it (insane practice btw...) or is Hanwha more directly tied with the US DoD?

let's look at the wikipedia page for Hanwha Vision:

Hanwha Vision (Korean: 한화비전), founded as Samsung Techwin, is a video surveillance company. It is a subsidiary of Hanwha Group.

Former products

K9 Thunder self-propelled artillery, K10 ammunition resupply vehicles, sub-systems for K2 Black Panther, sentry gun robot SGR-A1.

oh... okay.... I remember reading about the SGR-A1 when I was in high school, but I never thought I would be accidentally finding keys to the kingdom of the manufacturer on the ground later in my career... my life is kinda weird sometimes...

SPECULATION WARNING

even still, these aren't American devices, or anything like that. Why would Hanwha Vision need anything remotely related to the DoD? Is it possible that their CI is provided by some centralized team at their parent company Hanwha, where the needs of their sister company Hanwha Aerospace cause the shared platform to have these entries in the CI environment variables? Or maybe because of their other sister company, Hanwha Defense USA, where they make other large scary steel machines

I wanted to make sure this wasn't some kind of fluke, and that there weren't hundreds of other different github tokens in their firmware, so I scraped the Hanwha website to download every firmware for every camera i could find, and ended up with around ~500 firmwares (there were like 600 smth cameras but not all of them had firmware listed) and I was able to extract 62% of them with the same approach as above, and only three of them had github tokens, and they were all the same token.

not really sure why the others didn't work, but it's close enough for me to feel satisfied.

disclosure

I wrote up a very small email with enough information to identify where the token was and sent it over to Hanwha, who have a nice open email for reporting security issues, and they responded within 12 hours notifying me that the token had been revoked. Sure they shouldn't have ever had a gh token in there but I have never had such a prompt response and resolution.

we really gotta stop making these mistakes so often, how am I supposed to be sleeping at night?

thanks computer, until next time

I took a look at the site, and found that they had accessible firmware blobs for each model of camera, which is always a treat. poking and prodding i took the image and threw it at binwalk hoping it was just a rootfs or something, but inside there was a separate tarball with some AI stuff for the camera and a fwimage.tgz that binwalk was flagging as encrypted. I was googling around and saw that Matt Brown has a writeup on these cameras that got me through. basically the passphrase is HTW + the model number so HTWXNP-9300RW worked. seems like they do some more stuff now, because inside of that tarball was another fwimage.tgz that was encrypted but it wasn't the same scheme, so we can't just re-use the same setup from Matt Brown. I kinda figured I was gonna have to give up and that Hanwha was doing something more advanced, like burning a key into the hardware (which isnt foolproof obviously but you at least need to own the camera to start.) Anyways, there was a fwupgrader binary that was in that outer tarball, so I threw it into ghidra and started poking around. well I would have been poking around if it was 2023 or something, but i pointed claude code at it and went to make a lovely dinner and spent time with my partner instead, and came back a bit later to a description and a nice rootfs. I checked what repos the token had access to, and it had admin privileges to hundreds of repositories in their github organization. this isn't my first rodeo with an org shipping a Github token in their firmware though... but that's a story for a different blog post. Maybe it didn't get actually served and just lived on disk, though. there were some other... interesting bits of data in the environment though: there were some env vars with IP addresses in them, but they are assigned to the US Department of Defense: SWARM_MASTER_NFS_ADDRESS: 55.101.212.23 OTEL_ELASTIC_URL: http://55.101.212.21:5601/<snip> CIMIP: 55.101.211.213 huh... is this just a coincidence and one of those weird instances where people have taken IP space for internal services when they know they will never interact with it (insane practice btw...) or is Hanwha more directly tied with the US DoD? let's look at the wikipedia page for Hanwha Vision: Hanwha Vision (Korean: 한화비전), founded as Samsung Techwin, is a video surveillance company. It is a subsidiary of Hanwha Group. Former products K9 Thunder self-propelled artillery, K10 ammunition resupply vehicles, sub-systems for K2 Black Panther, sentry gun robot SGR-A1. oh... okay.... I remember reading about the SGR-A1 when I was in high school, but I never thought I would be accidentally finding keys to the kingdom of the manufacturer on the ground later in my career... my life is kinda weird sometimes... SPECULATION WARNING even still, these aren't American devices, or anything like that. Why would Hanwha Vision need anything remotely related to the DoD? Is it possible that their CI is provided by some centralized team at their parent company Hanwha, where the needs of their sister company Hanwha Aerospace cause the shared platform to have these entries in the CI environment variables? Or maybe because of their other sister company, Hanwha Defense USA, where they make other large scary steel machines I wanted to make sure this wasn't some kind of fluke, and that there weren't hundreds of other different github tokens in their firmware, so I scraped the Hanwha website to download every firmware for every camera i could find, and ended up with around ~500 firmwares (there were like 600 smth cameras but not all of them had firmware listed) and I was able to extract 62% of them with the same approach as above, and only three of them had github tokens, and they were all the same token. not really sure why the others didn't work, but it's close enough for me to feel satisfied. disclosure I wrote up a very small email with enough information to identify where the token was and sent it over to Hanwha, who have a nice open email for reporting security issues, and they responded within 12 hours notifying me that the token had been revoked. Sure they shouldn't have ever had a gh token in there but I have never had such a prompt response and resolution. we really gotta stop making these mistakes so often, how am I supposed to be sleeping at night? thanks computer, until next time

blocks.html_en: i have been thinking a bit more about security cameras again, because of AXIS starting to push more for every one of their cameras to be able to easily run linux applications on them, they're far more serious targets in an enterprise environment and need to be managed as such for vulnerabilities and credential management etc. someone brought up to me a company that sounded new to me, Hanwha (Vision.) I took a look at the site, and found that they had accessible firmware blobs for each model of camera, which is always a treat.

sentences.en: i have been thinking a bit more about security cameras again, because of AXIS starting to push more for every one of their cameras to be able to easily run linux applications on them, they're far more serious targets in an enterprise environment and need to be managed as such for vulnerabilities and credential management etc. someone brought up to me a company that sounded new to me, Hanwha (Vision.)
sentences.zh: 我再次对安全摄像头有了更多的思考，因为 AXIS 开始为他们的每一台摄像头提供更多支持，以便能够在其上轻松运行 Linux 应用程序，它们是企业环境中更严重的目标，需要对漏洞和凭证管理等进行管理。有人向我推荐了一家对我来说听起来很新的公司，Hanwha (Vision)。

sentences.en: I took a look at the site, and found that they had accessible firmware blobs for each model of camera, which is always a treat.
sentences.zh: 我浏览了该网站，发现他们为每种型号的相机提供了可访问的固件块，这总是一种享受。

blocks.html_en: poking and prodding

sentences.zh: 戳和戳

blocks.html_en: i took the image and threw it at binwalk hoping it was just a rootfs or something, but inside there was a separate tarball with some AI stuff for the camera and a fwimage.tgz that binwalk was flagging as encrypted.

sentences.en: i took the image and threw it at binwalk hoping it was just a rootfs or something, but inside there was a separate tarball with some AI stuff for the camera and a fwimage.tgz that binwalk was flagging as encrypted.
sentences.zh: 我拍下了这张图片，把它扔到 binwalk 上，希望它只是一个 rootfs 之类的东西，但里面有一个单独的 tarball，里面有一些用于相机的 AI 东西，还有一个 fwimage.tgz，binwalk 标记为加密的。

blocks.html_en: I was googling around and saw that Matt Brown has a writeup on these cameras that got me through. basically the passphrase is HTW + the model number so HTWXNP-9300RW worked.

sentences.en: I was googling around and saw that Matt Brown has a writeup on these cameras that got me through.
sentences.zh: 我在谷歌上搜索，看到马特·布朗有一篇关于这些相机的文章，帮助我完成了任务。

sentences.en: basically the passphrase is HTW + the model number so HTWXNP-9300RW worked.
sentences.zh: 基本上密码是 HTW + 型号，因此 HTWXNP-9300RW 可以工作。

blocks.html_en: seems like they do some more stuff now, because inside of that tarball was another fwimage.tgz that was encrypted but it wasn't the same scheme, so we can't just re-use the same setup from Matt Brown. I kinda figured I was gonna have to give up and that Hanwha was doing something more advanced, like burning a key into the hardware (which isnt foolproof obviously but you at least need to own the camera to start.) Anyways, there was a fwupgrader binary that was in that outer tarball, so I threw it into ghidra and started poking around.

sentences.en: seems like they do some more stuff now, because inside of that tarball was another fwimage.tgz that was encrypted but it wasn't the same scheme, so we can't just re-use the same setup from Matt Brown.
sentences.zh: 看起来他们现在做了更多的事情，因为该 tarball 内部是另一个加密的 fwimage.tgz，但它不是相同的方案，所以我们不能只是重复使用 Matt Brown 的相同设置。

sentences.en: I kinda figured I was gonna have to give up and that Hanwha was doing something more advanced, like burning a key into the hardware (which isnt foolproof obviously but you at least need to own the camera to start.)
sentences.zh: 我有点想我必须放弃，韩华正在做一些更先进的事情，比如将密钥烧入硬件（这显然不是万无一失的，但你至少需要拥有相机才能启动。）

sentences.en: Anyways, there was a fwupgrader binary that was in that outer tarball, so I threw it into ghidra and started poking around.
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，外面的 tarball 中有一个 fwupgrader 二进制文件，所以我把它扔到 ghidra 中并开始四处探索。

blocks.html_en: well I would have been poking around if it was 2023 or something, but i pointed claude code at it and went to make a lovely dinner and spent time with my partner instead, and came back a bit later to a description and a nice rootfs.

sentences.en: well I would have been poking around if it was 2023 or something, but i pointed claude code at it and went to make a lovely dinner and spent time with my partner instead, and came back a bit later to a description and a nice rootfs.
sentences.zh: 好吧，如果是 2023 年或者什么的，我会四处看看，但我指着克劳德代码，然后去做一顿美味的晚餐，并与我的搭档共度时光，稍后又回来查看描述和一个不错的 rootfs。

blocks.html_en: Hanwha had built some obfuscation into the fwupgrader to hide how they decrypt the actual rootfs. the AES key is XOR'd against a small static key table in the binary and reassembled at runtime ( the IV is just plaintext in there) the fwupgrader just shells out to the openssl CLI, and even the command fragments are XOR-obfuscated the same way.

sentences.en: Hanwha had built some obfuscation into the fwupgrader to hide how they decrypt the actual rootfs.
sentences.zh: 韩华在 fwupgrader 中内置了一些混淆功能，以隐藏他们如何解密实际的 rootfs。

sentences.en: the AES key is XOR'd against a small static key table in the binary and reassembled at runtime ( the IV is just plaintext in there) the fwupgrader just shells out to the openssl CLI, and even the command fragments are XOR-obfuscated the same way.
sentences.zh: AES 密钥与二进制文件中的一个小型静态密钥表进行异或，并在运行时重新组装（IV 只是其中的纯文本），fwupgrader 只是 shell 到 openssl CLI，甚至命令片段也以相同的方式进行异或混淆。

sentences.en: and with that we have a full rootfs to dig into normally.
sentences.zh: 这样我们就有了一个完整的 rootfs 可以正常挖掘。

sentences.en: truffles
sentences.zh: 松露

blocks.html_en: since we finally can just look at stuff I ran trufflehog immediately to see if there was anything obvious, and there was a github token duplicated in like 30 files... I checked what repos the token had access to, and it had admin privileges to hundreds of repositories in their github organization.

sentences.en: since we finally can just look at stuff I ran trufflehog immediately to see if there was anything obvious, and there was a github token duplicated in like 30 files...
sentences.zh: 因为我们终于可以看看东西了，所以我立即运行 trufflehog 来看看是否有任何明显的东西，并且有一个 github 令牌在大约 30 个文件中重复......

sentences.en: I checked what repos the token had access to, and it had admin privileges to hundreds of repositories in their github organization.
sentences.zh: 我检查了该令牌可以访问哪些存储库，并且它对 github 组织中的数百个存储库具有管理员权限。

blocks.html_en: this isn't my first rodeo with an org shipping a Github token in their firmware though... but that's a story for a different blog post. Why would this org put this token in like 30 files though? it looks like they build the UI for these cameras with vite, and one of the variables is being set to the entirety of process.env at build time, which means the entirety of the CI job's environment is being written to these files.

sentences.en: this isn't my first rodeo with an org shipping a Github token in their firmware though...
sentences.zh: 不过，这并不是我第一次与在其固件中发送 Github 令牌的组织进行竞技表演……

sentences.en: but that's a story for a different blog post.
sentences.zh: 但这是另一篇博客文章的故事了。

sentences.en: Why would this org put this token in like 30 files though?
sentences.zh: 为什么这个组织会把这个令牌放入大约 30 个文件中？

sentences.en: it looks like they build the UI for these cameras with vite, and one of the variables is being set to the entirety of process.env at build time, which means the entirety of the CI job's environment is being written to these files.
sentences.zh: 看起来他们使用 vite 为这些相机构建 UI，并且其中一个变量在构建时被设置为整个 process.env，这意味着 CI 作业的整个环境都被写入这些文件。

blocks.html_en: I don't have any of these cameras to test, but I think this would mean that anyone accessing the admin ui of these cameras likely has had this github token sent to them over the wire and (hopefully) nobody evil noticed. Maybe it didn't get actually served and just lived on disk, though.

sentences.en: I don't have any of these cameras to test, but I think this would mean that anyone accessing the admin ui of these cameras likely has had this github token sent to them over the wire and (hopefully) nobody evil noticed.
sentences.zh: 我没有任何这些相机可供测试，但我认为这意味着任何访问这些相机管理界面的人都可能已经通过网络将这个 github 令牌发送给他们，并且（希望）没有人注意到。

sentences.en: Maybe it didn't get actually served and just lived on disk, though.
sentences.zh: 不过，也许它并没有真正得到服务，而只是存在于磁盘上。

sentences.zh: 还有其他一些...

okay....
I remember reading about the SGR-A1 when I was in high school, but I never thought I would be accidentally finding keys to the kingdom of the manufacturer on the ground later in my career...
sentences.zh: 我记得在高中时读过有关 SGR-A1 的文章，但我从未想过在我的职业生涯后期会意外地在地面上找到通往制造商王国的钥匙......

sentences.en: my life is kinda weird sometimes...
sentences.zh: 我的生活有时有点奇怪...

blocks.html_en: SPECULATION WARNING

sentences.en: SPECULATION WARNING
sentences.zh: 投机警告

blocks.html_en: even still, these aren't American devices, or anything like that. Why would Hanwha Vision need anything remotely related to the DoD? Is it possible that their CI is provided by some centralized team at their parent company Hanwha, where the needs of their sister company Hanwha Aerospace cause the shared platform to have these entries in the CI environment variables? Or maybe because of their other sister company, Hanwha Defense USA, where they make other large scary steel machines

sentences.en: even still, these aren't American devices, or anything like that.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，这些设备也不是美国设备或类似设备。

sentences.en: Why would Hanwha Vision need anything remotely related to the DoD?
sentences.zh: 为什么韩华愿景需要任何与国防部远程相关的东西？

sentences.en: Is it possible that their CI is provided by some centralized team at their parent company Hanwha, where the needs of their sister company Hanwha Aerospace cause the shared platform to have these entries in the CI environment variables?
sentences.zh: 他们的 CI 是否有可能是由其母公司 Hanwha 的某个集中团队提供的，而其姊妹公司 Hanwha Aerospace 的需求导致共享平台在 CI 环境变量中包含这些条目？

sentences.en: Or maybe because of their other sister company, Hanwha Defense USA, where they make other large scary steel machines
sentences.zh: 或者也许是因为他们的另一家姐妹公司韩华防务美国公司，他们在那里制造其他大型可怕的钢铁机器

blocks.html_en: I wanted to make sure this wasn't some kind of fluke, and that there weren't hundreds of other different github tokens in their firmware, so I scraped the Hanwha website to download every firmware for every camera i could find, and ended up with around ~500 firmwares (there were like 600 smth cameras but not all of them had firmware listed) and I was able to extract 62% of them with the same approach as above, and only three of them had github tokens, and they were all the same token.

sentences.en: I wanted to make sure this wasn't some kind of fluke, and that there weren't hundreds of other different github tokens in their firmware, so I scraped the Hanwha website to download every firmware for every camera i could find, and ended up with around ~500 firmwares (there were like 600 smth cameras but not all of them had firmware listed) and I was able to extract 62% of them with the same approach as above, and only three of them had github tokens, and they were all the same token.
sentences.zh: 我想确保这不是某种侥幸，并且他们的固件中没有数百个其他不同的 github 令牌，因此我抓取了韩华网站，下载了我能找到的每台相机的每个固件，最终得到了大约 500 个固件（大约有 600 个相机，但并非所有相机都列出了固件），并且我能够使用与上述相同的方法提取其中的 62％，其中只有三个具有 github 令牌，并且它们是都是同一个令牌。

blocks.html_en: not really sure why the others didn't work, but it's close enough for me to feel satisfied.

sentences.en: not really sure why the others didn't work, but it's close enough for me to feel satisfied.
sentences.zh: 不太确定为什么其他人不起作用，但它已经足够接近让我感到满意了。

blocks.html_en: disclosure

sentences.en: disclosure
sentences.zh: 披露

blocks.html_en: I wrote up a very small email with enough information to identify where the token was and sent it over to Hanwha, who have a nice open email for reporting security issues, and they responded within 12 hours notifying me that the token had been revoked. Sure they shouldn't have ever had a gh token in there but I have never had such a prompt response and resolution.

sentences.en: I wrote up a very small email with enough information to identify where the token was and sent it over to Hanwha, who have a nice open email for reporting security issues, and they responded within 12 hours notifying me that the token had been revoked.
sentences.zh: 我写了一封非常小的电子邮件，其中包含足够的信息来识别令牌的位置，并将其发送给韩华，韩华有一封很好的公开电子邮件用于报告安全问题，他们在 12 小时内回复，通知我令牌已被撤销。

sentences.en: Sure they shouldn't have ever had a gh token in there but I have never had such a prompt response and resolution.
sentences.zh: 当然，他们不应该有 gh 令牌，但我从来没有得到过如此迅速的回应和解决。

blocks.html_en: we really gotta stop making these mistakes so often, how am I supposed to be sleeping at night?

sentences.en: we really gotta stop making these mistakes so often, how am I supposed to be sleeping at night?
sentences.zh: 我们真的不能再经常犯这些错误了，我晚上该怎么睡觉呢？

blocks.html_en: thanks computer, until next time

sentences.en: thanks computer, until next time
sentences.zh: 谢谢电脑，下次再见

content_html_zh:
我再次对安全摄像头有了更多的思考，因为 AXIS 开始为他们的每一台摄像头提供更多支持，以便能够在其上轻松运行 Linux 应用程序，它们是企业环境中更严重的目标，需要对漏洞和凭证管理等进行管理。有人向我推荐了一家对我来说听起来很新的公司，Hanwha (Vision)。 我浏览了该网站，发现他们为每种型号的相机提供了可访问的固件块，这总是一种享受。

戳和戳

我拍下了这张图片，把它扔到 binwalk 上，希望它只是一个 rootfs 之类的东西，但里面有一个单独的 tarball，里面有一些用于相机的 AI 东西，还有一个 fwimage.tgz，binwalk 标记为加密的。

我在谷歌上搜索，看到马特·布朗有一篇关于这些相机的文章，帮助我完成了任务。 基本上密码是 HTW + 型号，因此 HTWXNP-9300RW 可以工作。

看起来他们现在做了更多的事情，因为该 tarball 内部是另一个加密的 fwimage.tgz，但它不是相同的方案，所以我们不能只是重复使用 Matt Brown 的相同设置。 我有点想我必须放弃，韩华正在做一些更先进的事情，比如将密钥烧入硬件（这显然不是万无一失的，但你至少需要拥有相机才能启动。） 不管怎样，外面的 tarball 中有一个 fwupgrader 二进制文件，所以我把它扔到 ghidra 中并开始四处探索。

好吧，如果是 2023 年或者什么的，我会四处看看，但我指着克劳德代码，然后去做一顿美味的晚餐，并与我的搭档共度时光，稍后又回来查看描述和一个不错的 rootfs。

韩华在 fwupgrader 中内置了一些混淆功能，以隐藏他们如何解密实际的 rootfs。 AES 密钥与二进制文件中的一个小型静态密钥表进行异或，并在运行时重新组装（IV 只是其中的纯文本），fwupgrader 只是 shell 到 openssl CLI，甚至命令片段也以相同的方式进行异或混淆。

重建命令如下所示：

openssl enc -md sha256 -aes-256-cbc -d \
  -K <KEY> -iv <IV> -in <INPUT> -out <OUTPUT>

由于 key 和 iv 只是硬编码的（在整个模型系列中相同），我将在这里发布它们：

KEY = dfa049bb922e63e2decc764af5628068e5b7a2662e479a615b14643e567579b0
IV  = 53f926801b81454a4f889c9a390db6e6

这样我们就有了一个完整的 rootfs 可以正常挖掘。

松露

因为我们终于可以看看东西了，所以我立即运行 trufflehog 来看看是否有任何明显的东西，并且有一个 github 令牌在大约 30 个文件中重复...... 我检查了该令牌可以访问哪些存储库，并且它对 github 组织中的数百个存储库具有管理员权限。

不过，这并不是我第一次与在其固件中发送 Github 令牌的组织进行竞技表演…… 但这是另一篇博客文章的故事了。 为什么这个组织会把这个令牌放入大约 30 个文件中？ 看起来他们使用 vite 为这些相机构建 UI，并且其中一个变量在构建时被设置为整个 process.env，这意味着 CI 作业的整个环境都被写入这些文件。

var W = {
  DATAPORT: "9090",
  GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE: "1",
  npm_command: "run-script",
  KUBERNETES_SERVICE_PORT_HTTPS: "443",
  GITHUB_NPM_TOKEN: "<snip>:ghp_…REDACTED…",
  npm_config_userconfig: "/home/docker/.npmrc",
  // etc

我没有任何这些相机可供测试，但我认为这意味着任何访问这些相机管理界面的人都可能已经通过网络将这个 github 令牌发送给他们，并且（希望）没有人注意到。 不过，也许它并没有真正得到服务，而只是存在于磁盘上。

还有其他一些... 不过，环境中存在一些有趣的数据：有一些带有 IP 地址的环境变量，但它们被分配给美国国防部：

  • SWARM_MASTER_NFS_ADDRESS：55.101.212.23
  • OTEL_ELASTIC_URL：http://55.101.212.21:5601/<snip>
  • CIMIP: 55.101.211.213

呵呵... 这只是巧合吗？这是一种奇怪的情况，人们在知道自己永远不会与之交互的情况下却将 IP 空间用于内部服务（顺便说一句，这是疯狂的做法......） 还是韩华与美国国防部有更直接的联系？

我们来看看 Hanwha Vision 的维基百科页面：

Hanwha Vision（韩语：한화비전）的前身为 Samsung Techwin，是一家视频监控公司。 它是韩华集团的子公司。
前期产品有K9“雷霆”自行火炮、K10弹药补给车、K2“黑豹”子系统、哨兵火炮机器人SGR-A1。

哦... 好的.... 我记得在高中时读过有关 SGR-A1 的文章，但我从未想过在我的职业生涯后期会意外地在地面上找到通往制造商王国的钥匙...... 我的生活有时有点奇怪...

投机警告

即便如此，这些设备也不是美国设备或类似设备。 为什么韩华愿景需要任何与国防部远程相关的东西？ 他们的 CI 是否有可能是由其母公司 Hanwha 的某个集中团队提供的，而其姊妹公司 Hanwha Aerospace 的需求导致共享平台在 CI 环境变量中包含这些条目？ 或者也许是因为他们的另一家姐妹公司韩华防务美国公司，他们在那里制造其他大型可怕的钢铁机器

我想确保这不是某种侥幸，并且他们的固件中没有数百个其他不同的 github 令牌，因此我抓取了韩华网站，下载了我能找到的每台相机的每个固件，最终得到了大约 500 个固件（大约有 600 个相机，但并非所有相机都列出了固件），并且我能够使用与上述相同的方法提取其中的 62％，其中只有三个具有 github 令牌，并且它们是都是同一个令牌。

不太确定为什么其他人不起作用，但它已经足够接近让我感到满意了。

披露

我写了一封非常小的电子邮件，其中包含足够的信息来识别令牌的位置，并将其发送给韩华，韩华有一封很好的公开电子邮件用于报告安全问题，他们在 12 小时内回复，通知我令牌已被撤销。 当然，他们不应该有 gh 令牌，但我从来没有得到过如此迅速的回应和解决。

我们真的不能再经常犯这些错误了，我晚上该怎么睡觉呢？

谢谢电脑，下次再见
